# UC-22.2.4 ┬Ę NIS2 Business Continuity and Crisis Management Monitoring

## Description

Correlates ServiceNow GRC and BCM task evidence with a BCP inventory lookup and ITSI service-health rows during declared drills, surfacing overdue plan reviews, missing failover test dates, stalled workflow states, and services that breach health expectations while `is_drill=1`.

## Value

Instead of chasing spreadsheets before an audit, executives get a dated list of which continuity artefacts are out of cadence and which practice runs actually stressed the production service tree ŌĆö so gaps are fixed before regulators ask and crisis calls start with facts, not guesses.

## Implementation

(1) Install Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) and enable the GRC/policy and task pulls your process already uses; map `category=bcp_review` for BCM work. (2) Load `nis2_bcp_inventory.csv` via Lookup Editor or `lookups/` + `transforms.conf`. (3) Tag ITSI entities used in failover drills; ensure `itsi:service_health` includes `is_drill` during exercises. (4) Schedule the saved search weekly and push results to the compliance dashboard. (5) Archive outputs with RFC3161 timestamping per `evidenceSigning`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
ŌĆó Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search tier with role `nis2_bcp_reader` granting `index=itsm`, `index=itsi`, and `srchLookups` for `nis2_bcp_inventory`.
ŌĆó Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) Ōēź8.x on a Heavy Forwarder or data collection node with OAuth / basic credential stored in the credential store; enable modular inputs that hydrate `sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy` and `sourcetype=snow:task` at Ōēż15 minute intervals for BCM categories.
ŌĆó Splunk IT Service Intelligence (Splunkbase 1841) with Service Analyzer access; drill tagging convention `is_drill=1` on `sourcetype=itsi:service_health` rows for the regulated entity tree (configure via ITSI episode policy or alert aggregation during exercises).
ŌĆó CSV lookup `lookups/nis2_bcp_inventory.csv` deployed through the shared compliance app with `transforms.conf` stanza `[nis2_bcp_inventory]` and `check_matches = warn` during QA.

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
In `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_snow/local/inputs.conf`, duplicate the vendor template stanzas for GRC policy tables and `sc_task` / `task` pulls, setting `index = itsm`, `polling_interval = 300`, and `start_by_shell = false` as recommended in the TA docs. Map table columns to `plan_id`, `state`, `category`, and `cmdb_ci` via `props.conf` FIELDALIAS so the SPL `plan_key` coalesce resolves consistently. For ITSI, validate KV store collections `itsi_group_entities` include CMDB keys that line up with `plan_key`. Run `splunk cmd splunkd print-modinput-config --serveruri https://localhost:8089 Splunk_TA_snow://<input>` from the CLI to confirm the modular input returns HTTP 200 and JSON payloads under 5 MB per poll.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search, test panel, and alert
Use Search & Reporting ŌåÆ New Search, paste the UC SPL, set time range Last 180 days, then Save As ŌåÆ Alert with cron `0 7 * * MON` and throttle 24 hours on `plan_key`. Add a drilldown that opens `ServiceNow -> GRC -> Policies` filtered by `plan_key` and a second drill that opens ITSI **Service Analyzer** for `service_name`. Understanding this SPL: the outer search isolates BCM artefacts; `lookup nis2_bcp_inventory` supplies contractual review cadence; `eval review_breach` turns policy data into a boolean; the `append` subsearch pulls only drill-window health rows so failover evidence is not drowned by steady-state noise.

Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
Pick three plans from the CSV and manually confirm `last_review_date` in ServiceNow matches the Splunk row within one business day. Run `| makeresults | eval plan_key="TEST-PLAN-01" | lookup nis2_bcp_inventory plan_id AS plan_key OUTPUT *` to verify the lookup path. In ITSI, execute a coordinated drill, watch `index=itsi sourcetype=itsi:service_health is_drill=1 earliest=-4h` in Search, and confirm severity lines up with the Service Analyzer tile for the same `service_name`.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize & Troubleshoot
Publish a Dashboard Studio board with tokens for `plan_owner` and `service_name`. Wire an adaptive response (email or webhook) that embeds the RFC3161 token reference produced by your evidence pipeline. Weekly governance: export the panel to PDF, store in the records tool, and attach the timestamp manifest.

Step 5 ŌĆö Troubleshooting
ŌĆó **No `snow:sn_grc_policy` events** ŌĆö input disabled or wrong endpoint: check **Settings ŌåÆ Data inputs ŌåÆ Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow**, validate REST path `/api/now/table/sn_grc_policy`, rotate API credentials if HTTP 401 appears in `splunkd.log`.
ŌĆó **Lookup misses every row** ŌĆö casing mismatch on `plan_id`; normalize with `| eval plan_key=lower(plan_key)` before lookup or rebuild CSV keys.
ŌĆó **`append` returns nothing** ŌĆö ITSI macro not setting `is_drill=1`; temporarily remove `is_drill=1` filter, confirm `sourcetype=itsi:service_health` exists, then re-enable tagging via episode workflow.
ŌĆó **False overdue reviews** ŌĆö CSV `review_cycle_days` stale; refresh the file and bump `last_updated` column tracked in a helper search.
ŌĆó **RBAC errors** ŌĆö user missing `itsm` or `itsi` index: add indexes to the role via **Settings ŌåÆ Access controls ŌåÆ Roles**.

## SPL

```spl
(index=itsm (sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy OR (sourcetype=snow:task category=bcp_review)) earliest=-180d@d) OR (index=itsi sourcetype=itsi:service_health is_drill=1 earliest=-24h)
| eval plan_key=if(index="itsm",coalesce(plan_id,u_bcp_plan_ref,cmdb_ci),service_name)
| lookup nis2_bcp_inventory plan_id AS plan_key OUTPUT plan_name, owner, review_cycle_days, last_review_date, last_test_date
| eval last_review_epoch=if(isnotnull(last_review_date),strptime(last_review_date,"%Y-%m-%d"),null())
| eval days_since_review=if(index="itsm" AND isnotnull(last_review_epoch),round((now()-last_review_epoch)/86400,1),null())
| eval review_breach=0
| eval review_breach=if(index="itsm" AND ((days_since_review>review_cycle_days) OR isnull(last_test_date) OR match(lower(state),"pending|waiting|work in progress")),1,review_breach)
| eval review_breach=if(index="itsi" AND NOT match(service_health_status,"(?i)normal|green"),1,review_breach)
| where review_breach=1
| stats latest(service_health_status) as svc_status latest(days_since_review) as days_since_review latest(owner) as plan_owner values(plan_name) as plans by plan_key index
| sort 0 - days_since_review
```

## Visualization

Dashboard Studio row A: table of `plan_key`, `plan_owner`, `days_since_review`, BIA freshness flag from lookup. Row B: ITSI Service Analyzer deep link for drill services in breach. Row C: single value of open `snow:task` counts stuck >14 days.
